intro: JavaScript's dynamic scripting works well for highly decoupled web interfaces but can become error-prone in enterprise environments. Transitioning to C# provides a robust, type-safe, component-driven architecture managed by Microsoft's .NET platform. This migration path helps engineering teams implement explicit software design patterns, maintain codebase stability at scale, and maximize backend compute efficiency.

diffs.from: Single-threaded event loop handling asynchronous logic.
diffs.to: Multi-threaded execution engine backed by a generational garbage collector.

diffs.from: Promise engines driven by macro/micro task scheduling.
diffs.to: Task Parallel Library (TPL) utilizing thread pool task handoffs.
Strict class-based single inheritance with interface support.

examples.title: Asynchronous Task Processing
examples.description: Executing a delayed background operations without freezing primary processing threads.

examples.title: Data Collection Mapping
examples.description: Transforming values inside array containers cleanly.
examples.fromCode:
const source = [1, 2, 3];
const double = source.map(x => x * 2);

tips.title: Apply LINQ Extensions
tips.body: Replace array array transformations like map and filter with C# LINQ syntax (Select and Where).

tips.title: Configure Access Visibility
tips.body: Explicitly mark classes and methods as public or internal, as C# defaults to private access.

tips.title: Handle Nullable References
tips.body: Enable nullable reference tracking to intercept potential null reference exceptions before compilation.

tips.title: Enforce Explicit Type Declarations
tips.body: Use explicit type declarations or the "var" keyword for local variables when the assigned type is clear.
